inline_comments.message: I think ModuleEntity should also implement Serializable.
Remove unused import.
Change to private

I think the last time I talked to Tony about this he recommended to return 'the id the users needs to get the object' which is the user defined anchorName in this case
(I know the user supplied it himself but still its good to stick to the pattern of retuning an ide upon create)

I know these are onyl a few lines but it might be good to separate methods 
like
- verifyDataSacepExists(...)
- verifyMoudleExists(...)
same module revision can be added to more than one dataspace so you need to add datapace_id to the constraint too
not sure if the order really matter here but logically I would like to see the dataspace_id first as it is the 'highest level' entity
Not sure what good this index is...

dependency injection via constructor is more preferable.
it allows making fields final, clearly instructs the components 
initialization order, simplifies unit testing etc.

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40620000/spring-autowire-on-properties-vs-constructor

i'd suggest revert this change

I think we decided not to return anything. Remind me if i am wrong. If we do return, it should be actaul id, but sice we decided not to expose that, returning anchor name does not make sense to me. Open for discussion.....
yes, all entities should, I changed the ones used in this review. Also, I remember giving a comment to someone to rename ModuleEntity to Module. I can do that as a separate commit.
if some other method in future needs it, will refactor then.

This has been a debate ever since spring launched, i prefer setter injection only as a matter of style. There are loads of blogs supporting each side. If others prefer constructor, i'd be happy to change.
i think this was added as part of Aditya's code, I just moved it up. Please create a separate task as this is not relevant to this story.

I usually go with constructor. But not strong attachment, I open to any option as long we keep consistency among the project
remove final from interfaces
same here
I agree, it should be consistent across the project. let's see what Bruno and Toine think. I propose setter injection.
my IDE auto adds it for me, removed now.

what about use modelmapper

https://www.baeldung.com/entity-to-and-from-dto-for-a-java-spring-application

ModelMapper is a good suggestion. Though I do not want to use it and explicitly create DTOs here for our service layer, which will be pretty standard and will form part of our API. Model mapper will tie it to a entity and its datatypes, hence introduce coupling, which we all do not want. Entities  might mostly be the same(unless we use something specific to a DB like JSONB in postresql) for RDBMS but may change in case of some NoSQL Db. If we introduce a interface above the Entities, then this can be considered again.
since we decided to phase out ConstraintViolationException from CpsRestExceptionHandler.java earlier today

you will be using modelmapper to map Anchor to AnchorDetails, same as you do here.
but decoupling the responsability of mapping from CpsRestController. Not mapping to the entity.
the discussion I had with Tony as after that, just to re-iterator Tony and I would like to return the id used by the client in this case the anchorName
the discussion I had with Tony as after that, just to re-iterator Tony and I would like to return the id used by the client in this case the anchorName
I would prefer if you do it now...

I don't have a strong opinion, only thing I can add is that setter injection does not give us problems with testing as Groovy is well able to override private variables (we have already doen this in some tests)

we came to realize it as part of your user story (and missed in aditya's story which is already closed) lets just be pragmatic and update it here too now.
You can also see it is making it consistent we the unique contraints for anchors.

OK< I'll Create a user story to revisit the DB schema, indexes etc in a little while.
See https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-81
does not make difference for compiler, if your IDE keeps doing it I wouldn't worry about it
OK. Do you want it only in REST or Java API as well?

The behavior to return boolean or throw exception in newly created method is very use case specific andd hence the method(of just a single line) may not be so useful.So, if in future we do drift towards one approach or the other would make sense to add method then to avoid rework. If you still want this , let me know which approach is your preference and i will happily implement.
Done
good question, yes I think Java API should behave the same

these method try to grow over time. Even if small now it is good to describe exactly what is happening by having descriptive method names and even if now they only contain one line
Void method that throw an exception is fine in this case.

This is really about guiding future developers to keep everything nice and tidy.

Thanks,Toine

e.g. Anchor with name xyx already exisst in dataspace abc

Consider moving findModule to moduleRepository service and call it getModule
Same for Dataspace method
Keep responsibilities decoupled, module service should take care of get / find or save, or anything related to module. Same for dataspace service.
So where *FIND* method should get maybe an optional, *GET* gets the object or throws and exception. And downstream consumers doesn't need to care about implementation, neither will be duplicated code if different services needs to do some GET for example
missing final
Consider add "Not Found" "Duplication Error" as public Static Variables under CpsNotFoundException, so it can be reused

The dataspaceRepository does have an exists method curerntly, how about we use that pattern, exists and then find instead of optional (though i commented against it previously)? My vote is for using optional and I did discuss this previously but optional was downvoted. lemme know which is preferred approach by the group.

Duplication error is used with cpsvalidationerror(currently). I think Ruslan introduced an Enum in one of his patches for this same purpose, not sure why it didn't make it to the merge.

pre-existing code. just added final. Will change anyhow.
as more verbose less readable I will say, also I will say doesnt provide valuable information in this case

I think there is space for each case, and each is valid and useful.

- get -> returns object or exception
- find -> returns optional
- exist -> boolean

sure. will leave the test code comments till our chat

there is no need in 'throws' for runtime exception (+ same in a neighbor method).

also it seems reasonable to move actual exception throwing onto upper level.
to avoid the case when "not found" exception is thrown for "insert" requests.
see comment for CpServiceImplSpec.groovy. this seems to require some discussion.

returning "not found" error on "create" request seems to be wrong behavior.
it's not a "fetch" request, when data is expected in response.
in this case providing invalid reference for new record is bad input.
so validation exception ("bad request" response) seems more reasonable here.

I am not sure will we have a repository and a service for each of our type of objects?! 
I don't see the need for such fine granularity, please ask other reviewers to commit on this.
If we do decide to have a ...Service for each object DataPerssitencyService need to refactors as no such class then probably will exists anymore...
There is something not quite right with teh exception handling and teh way it is tested now, please address Ruslan's comments on that first and then we can look at teh test again
this is responsibility of the repository, check my comment on DataspaceRepository
rebase and align this file.

Why are converting CpsNotFoundException to CpsValidationException?

default Dataspace getByName(String name) throws CpsNotFoundException
Also I see the other repo uses @nonnulll annotations, try to keep consistency here as well
add CpsNotFoundException
is there some reason for the change of the order? same for the others lines, try to reduce the changes required to the minimum and related to the task of this commit
Were added previously. See previous comments from Ruslan.

See previous comments from Ruslan. he said a create request should not throw a NOtFound exception. The repository get request will still throw a notfound, but this method makes sense to throw a validation exception. Hence the conversion.
Done
see similar comment
Have tried to move the ID to the top as it was buried within other fields and hard to read.

ok, but createAnchor should throw CpsNotFoundException  or DataIntegrityViolationException,
and consumer handle the exceptions. CpsNotFoundException to CpsValidationException conversions shouldnt be done here but instead on CpServiceImpl

i think consumer should not be exposed to DataIntegrityViolationException as it is RDBMS based. As ruslan said, create request should not throw CpsNotFoundException. This class is fully responsible for saving the anchor so i think this is the right place for it. THis can be discussed.

not sure if there is a need to repeat the parameter names in the method name.  I think it looks a bit ugly and will quickly lead to inconsistencies.
I think it fine if the method signature clarifies the intent like

Make dataspace the first parameter as it is the 'highest level'
This should be consistently be applied to all Java api methods and internal methods
